heading: Body landmarks, every frame
text: Body landmarks, every frame. MediaPipe BlazePose detects 33 points in world space — hips, knees, elbows, ears, the full skeleton. Tracked at full webcam framerate, hip-centered, in meters.

heading: Peer-reviewed thresholds
text: Peer-reviewed thresholds. Every detector's angle thresholds trace back to research on joint range and injury risk. Not guessed, not generic — pulled from the literature, validated frame by frame.

heading: One detector per exercise
text: One detector per exercise. Squat logic doesn't share code with deadlift. Each exercise has its own state machine, its own form checks, its own idea of what 'good' means. Specialised, not general.

heading: Home
text: . No nagging, no cheerleading, no constant affirmations. Just space to train.

heading: Home
text: Real-time AI feedback on your exercise technique. Ten exercises, thirty-three body landmarks, one trainer that never looks away.

heading: Home
text: A good coach doesn't yell. Doesn't recite numbers. Doesn't pile on corrections while you're mid-rep. FORMA was designed with the same restraint.

heading: Home
text: Real-time form feedback is only the start. FORMA remembers your sessions, talks back when you ask, writes you training plans, and opens up its own internals when you want to see how it thinks.

heading: Specialised beats general
text: Specialised beats general. A purpose-built state machine with the right angle thresholds will outperform a general-purpose ML model on a feedback task — every time. FORMA uses dedicated detectors per exercise, not a single model trying to cover all 10.

heading: Silence is a feature
text: Silence is a feature. Constant feedback is noise. FORMA only speaks when something is wrong. If your form is holding, the app stays out of your way. A good coach doesn't narrate every rep.

heading: Honesty over flattery
text: Honesty over flattery. No vague encouragement. No false positives. Green means your form holds. Red means you need to fix it. If FORMA can't see clearly, it says so — it doesn't guess.

heading: About
text: Google's real-time pose estimator. 33 landmarks, world coordinates, visibility per point.

heading: About
text: Per-exercise state machines and form checks. No ML at inference — deterministic, fast, debuggable.

heading: About
text: Streams webcam frames to the detector and pushes feedback back to the browser in real time.

heading: About
text: TypeScript, Tailwind 4, Framer Motion, Lenis smooth scroll. Editorial typography and scroll-driven motion.

heading: About
text: FORMA is a BSc Computer Science final-year project at the University of Greenwich. It started with a simple question:

heading: About
text: You can film yourself, but you don't know what to look for. You can ask a trainer, but trainers cost money and aren't in your living room at 7am. You can guess from YouTube tutorials, but nothing tells you

heading: About
text: So the project became this: take the hardware everyone already has — a webcam — and build a trainer out of it. One that watches every landmark on every frame, knows what good form looks like, and tells you the moment something breaks.

heading: About
text: Everything you've read is running in the browser right now. Press the button.

heading: Webcam frame
text: Webcam frame. Captured at full webcam framerate. No special hardware, no wearables, no markers. Any laptop with a camera works — FORMA meets you where you already train.

heading: MediaPipe BlazePose
text: MediaPipe BlazePose. Google's pose estimator tracks 33 body landmarks in real time, with world-space coordinates and a confidence score per point.

heading: Joint angles
text: Joint angles. Vector math turns raw landmarks into biomechanical angles — knee flexion, hip hinge, elbow extension, spine neutrality.

heading: Dedicated detector
text: Dedicated detector. A state machine per exercise: TOP → GOING_DOWN → BOTTOM → GOING_UP → TOP. No shared logic between squat and deadlift.

heading: Form score
text: Form score. A weighted score comes out of the detector every frame, based on the form checks that matter for that specific exercise.

heading: Feedback
text: Feedback. Words, not numbers. "Hips dropping." "Go a little deeper." Spoken through the browser's own voice when the rep needs it.

heading: How It Works
text: Each frame is grabbed directly from the browser's MediaStream. No upload, no buffer, no round-trip.

heading: How It Works
text: BlazePose runs fully in the browser via WebAssembly. The model weights ship with the page; nothing is fetched at runtime.

heading: How It Works
text: Three-point angles computed per joint, filtered through a small rolling window to smooth jitter without introducing lag.

heading: How It Works
text: Each detector knows what bad form looks like for its specific exercise. Squat watches knee depth and torso lean. Bench watches elbow flare and bar path. The thresholds come from peer-reviewed biomechanics.

heading: How It Works
text: Green when your alignment holds. Orange when it slips. Red when something needs immediate correction — so you always know where you stand without reading numbers.

heading: How It Works
text: One correction at a time. Silence when you're good. FORMA only speaks when something is wrong — a good coach doesn't narrate every rep.

heading: How It Works
text: A full pipeline pass — capture, pose estimation, angle math, detection, scoring, feedback — completes in under 50 ms on a consumer laptop. That means you see the correction before you finish the rep.

heading: How It Works
text: Nothing round-trips to a server. Nothing waits in a queue. The entire loop lives on your machine, in your browser, running at webcam framerate.

heading: How It Works
text: No video is uploaded. No frames are sent to a server. No silhouettes, no biometric templates, no cloud copy of your session. Pose estimation runs locally; the only thing the backend ever sees is the numeric session summary you choose to save.

heading: How It Works
text: When FORMA can't see clearly, it says so — it doesn't guess. When it doesn't need to speak, it stays quiet. Trust is built by doing less, not more.

heading: How It Works
text: Every exercise gets its own state machine and its own form checks. A squat detector does not share logic with a deadlift detector. Specialised beats general, every time.

heading: Every joint,\nevery frame.
text: Every joint,\nevery frame.. MediaPipe BlazePose tracks 33 body landmarks in world-space at the speed of your webcam. Each frame goes through angle computation, state machine, detector — all in under 50 ms.

heading: Specialised, not\ngeneral.
text: Specialised, not\ngeneral.. A squat detector watches knee depth and torso lean. A bench detector watches elbow flare and bar path. No one-size-fits-all ML model — each exercise gets its own state machine and its own thresholds.

heading: Words,\nnot numbers.
text: Words,\nnot numbers.. Green when your alignment holds. Orange when it slips. Red when something needs immediate correction. One cue at a time — never a wall of text — and silence when you're good.

heading: Tell it your goal.\nGet a plan.
text: Tell it your goal.\nGet a plan.. Set a goal — volume, frequency, a target lift — and FORMA generates a plan that adapts as you progress. Auto-generated milestones at 25, 50, 75, 100 percent. No spreadsheet.

heading: Progress you\ncan feel.
text: Progress you\ncan feel.. Thirteen badges, each tied to a real achievement — first session, first ten sessions, first perfect form score, first 100-rep day. Celebration moments that aren't patronising, because they're earned.

text: A guide for\nfirst-time visitors.. New here? The floating guide at the bottom-right answers questions about FORMA itself — what it does, what you need, how privacy works. It reads from an ingested knowledge base of this site.

heading: Your body,\nyour machine.
text: Your body,\nyour machine.. No video uploaded. No frames sent to a server. No silhouettes, no biometric templates, no cloud copy. Pose estimation runs locally in your browser — the only thing the backend ever sees is the numeric summary you choose to save.

heading: Features
text: One product, built around the way you actually train. Pose detection to plans, dashboards to dedicated detectors — nine pieces that work the same way every session.

heading: Features
text: Everything on this page is running in the browser right now. Press the button.

heading: What is FORMA
text: FORMA is a real-time computer vision form coach for ten foundational exercises: squat, deadlift, bench press, overhead press, lunge, pull-up, push-up, plank, bicep curl, tricep dip. It runs in your browser with just a webcam — no wearables, no installs, no uploads. Pose estimation is done on-device via MediaPipe BlazePose, and each exercise has its own dedicated detector that scores your form in real time.

heading: What you need
text: You need a laptop or desktop with a webcam and a modern browser. No app install, no special hardware, no fitness tracker. FORMA works with your laptop's built-in camera.

heading: Privacy and data
text: FORMA processes your webcam frames locally in the browser. Nothing is uploaded — no video, no frames, no biometric templates. The only things that ever reach the backend are the numeric session summaries you choose to save, and only when you are signed in.

heading: How to start
text: Click Sign In in the top-right, then switch to the Create Account tab. After signing up you land on the dashboard, where you can pick an exercise and start a session immediately.

heading: Main features
text: FORMA ships with nine main features: real-time pose detection, ten dedicated per-exercise detectors, form score with live feedback cues, drill-down dashboard and session history, adaptive plans and goals, milestones and badges, an AI coach that knows your training history, a floating website guide for visitors, and on-device processing for privacy.
